slides.title: What Are Natural Resources?
slides.content:
The Earth gives us many things we need to live. These are called natural resources.

Sunlight - helps plants grow and gives us energy
Water - we drink, cook, and wash with it
Wood - comes from trees, used for building and paper

Natural resources come from nature, not from factories!
slides.tip: Natural = from nature. Resources = things we use.

slides.title: Trash and Landfills
slides.content:
Sometimes people are not careful with resources.

People throw away a lot of trash. Trash is rubbish - things we don't want anymore.

The trash goes to a landfill. A landfill is a big area of land full of rubbish.

Landfills are very bad for the Earth. They take up space and pollute the ground.

slides.title: Smoke and Pollution
slides.content:
Cars and factories make smoke. Smoke makes the air dirty.

Dirty air is bad for people and animals. It can make us ill.

We can help by walking or cycling instead of going by car!

slides.title: Reduce and Reuse
slides.content:
We can help the Earth in two important ways:

Reduce = use LESS. Turn off the lights. Use less water. Don't waste food.

Reuse = use things AGAIN. Use a water bottle again. Give old toys to friends. Use both sides of paper.

If we all reduce and reuse, we can take care of our planet!
slides.tip: Reduce = less. Reuse = again. Two easy ways to help!

slides.title: Prepositions of Place
slides.content:
Prepositions of place tell us WHERE something is.

In front of - the tree is in front of the house
Behind - the garden is behind the house
Next to - the shop is next to the school
Across from - the park is across from the library
Between - the cat is between the two boxes
slides.tip: Prepositions answer the question: WHERE is it?

slides.title: Example: Prepositions of Place
slides.problem:
Look at the picture in your mind: a tent, a campfire, and tall trees.

Fill in the blanks:
1. The campfire is _____ the tent.
2. The tall trees are _____ the tent.
3. The duck is swimming _____ the two rocks.
steps: 1. The campfire is IN FRONT OF the tent.
2. The tall trees are BEHIND the tent.
3. The duck is swimming BETWEEN the two rocks.

slides.title: Where Is...? Is There...?
slides.content:
We use Where is...? to ask about the location of something.

'Where is the park?' - 'It is next to the school.'

We use Is there...? to ask if something exists in a place.

'Is there a park near your house?' - 'Yes, there is!' or 'No, there isn't.'

slides.title: Example: Where Is / Is There
slides.problem:
Complete with Where is or Is there:

1. _____ the library?
2. _____ a swimming pool near here?
3. _____ your house?
steps: 1. WHERE IS the library? (asking for location)
2. IS THERE a swimming pool near here? (asking if it exists)
3. WHERE IS your house? (asking for location)
slides.answer: Where is, Is there, Where is

slides.title: A Day in Nature
slides.html:
Last Saturday, the family went camping! They set up the tent next to a beautiful river.

The campfire was in front of the tent. The tall trees were behind them. A little bridge was across from the campsite.

'Where is the best place to swim?' asked Tom. 'The lake is between those two hills,' said Dad.

'Is there a bin for our trash?' asked Mum. 'We must reduce our rubbish and reuse our bottles!'

They picked up all their trash before leaving. They love nature and want to take care of it!

slides.title: Our School Garden
slides.html:
'Is there a garden at your school?' asked the teacher. 'Yes, there is! It's behind the gym.'

The children went to the garden. The flowers were in front of the vegetable patch. The compost bin was next to the shed.

'Where is the watering can?' asked Sara. 'It's between the two plant pots,' said the teacher.

The children learned about natural resources. Plants need sunlight and water to grow. Wood comes from trees. We should reduce waste and reuse things to help the Earth!

slides.title: Let's Review!
points: 🌿 Natural resources: sunlight, water, wood - things from nature we use
🗑️ Trash goes to landfills - big areas of land full of rubbish
🏭 Smoke from cars and factories pollutes the air
♻️ Reduce = use less, Reuse = use again
📍 Prepositions: in front of, behind, next to, across from, between
❓ Where is...? = asks for location. Is there...? = asks if it exists
